scope: errors/... encoding/... log/... metadata/... config/... selector/... internal/...
rationale: A narrower, lower-risk slice limited to pure-library subtrees with minimal external dependencies (no grpc/http server stacks). Maximizes the chance of a green build+test if the full //... pulls heavy transitive deps or hits a stray test failure.
module_bazel:
module(
    name = "kratos",
    version = "0.0.0",
)

bazel_dep(name = "rules_go", version = "0.61.1")
bazel_dep(name = "gazelle", version = "0.51.3")
bazel_dep(name = "rules_proto", version = "7.1.0")
bazel_dep(name = "protobuf", version = "35.1")

go_sdk = use_extension("@rules_go//go:extensions.bzl", "go_sdk")
go_sdk.download(version = "1.25.4")

go_deps = use_extension("@gazelle//:extensions.bzl", "go_deps")
go_deps.from_file(go_mod = "//:go.mod")
root_build:
load("@gazelle//:def.bzl", "gazelle")

# gazelle:prefix github.com/go-kratos/kratos/v3
# gazelle:exclude cmd
# gazelle:exclude contrib
# gazelle:exclude third_party
# gazelle:exclude docs
# gazelle:exclude transport
# gazelle:exclude middleware
# gazelle:exclude registry
gazelle(name = "gazelle")
anticipated_risks: internal/testdata proto .pb.go consumed by encoding/form tests — committed so fine
config/file_test uses filesystem testdata fixtures (runfiles path differences)
some internal tests may use net/host bindings that fail in sandbox
